prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "侧平板抬腿" (side plank with leg raise) exercise, side profile view of the lateral edge of the body, the body horizontal and supported on one forearm only. Person posture: Athletic person holding a side plank on the LEFT side with the top leg lifted: the body is LYING ON ITS LEFT SIDE (lateral side, NOT supine, NOT prone). Only the LEFT FOREARM is on the ground (left elbow directly under left shoulder, left forearm perpendicular to the body), and ONLY the OUTER EDGE of the LEFT FOOT touches the ground supporting the body — these are the only two contact points. The body is held in one perfectly straight rigid line from the left foot through the hips to the head, parallel to the ground. Hips are lifted off the ground (not sagging). The RIGHT (top) leg is lifted upward to about 30-45 degrees above horizontal, knee fully extended. The right arm is fully extended STRAIGHT UP toward the ceiling, perpendicular to the body line. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. CRITICAL: this is a SIDE PLANK position — body sideways with one forearm support — NOT a four-limb extended X-pose, NOT a supine pose, NOT a face-down pose.. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: obliques and gluteus medius of both hips (top hip for abduction, bottom hip for stability). Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: quadratus lumborum and shoulder stabilizers. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "腹斜肌" pointing to the obliques; "臀中肌" pointing to the gluteus medius of both hips; "腰方肌" pointing to the quadratus lumborum. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
